generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance TemperatureControlActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "A heating element integrated into the styrofoam box to regulate internal temperature via embedded controller, interfaced with Digital Twin algorithms for real-time adjustments"
]

instance AirflowActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "A motorized airflow distributor within the styrofoam box, actuated to optimize thermal distribution via DT-generated supervisory logic"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PermafrostTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometers embedded within the styrofoam box for temperature measurement, ensuring uniform spatial coverage"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PermafrostTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PhysicalThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical thermal model representing temperature dynamics within the styrofoam enclosure, refined via Python (Scipy)-based calibration algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonCalibrationEnabler
]

instance StateEstimationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic state estimation model using sensor data to infer hidden parameters (e.g., internal temperature gradients) for improved model accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonCalibrationEnabler
]

instance SimulationEngineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation engine model reproducing PT behavior under dynamic conditions, leveraging implicit simulation frameworks within the DT framework"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQCommunicationEnabler
]

instance TemperatureDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time numerical temperature data stream collected from PermafrostTemperatureSensor via 1-Wire protocol, formatted as time-series readings"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonCalibrationEnabler, RabbitMQCommunicationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ-based communication layer facilitating distributed data processing between sensors and DT components, deployed on Raspberry Pi 4 Model B"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, DataIntegrationService
]

instance PythonCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python (Scipy)-based calibration algorithms updating parameter estimates in thermal and state estimation models for improved model fidelity"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelRefinementService, StateEstimationService
]

instance ControlInterfaceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi acting as control interface executing DT-generated supervisory logic for actuator commands"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of PT performance via sensor data, detecting deviations from optimal behavior for engineers/operators"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceDeviationInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis optimizing system performance (energy efficiency, operational cost) under current conditions for engineers/decision-makers"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulating and forecasting PT behavior under hypothetical scenarios (stress tests, environmental changes) for designers/maintenance teams"
    DTDFVocab:provides BehaviorForecastInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated/human-driven interventions (parameter adjustments, repairs) when violations detected via supervisory logic"
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuatorCommandAction, SafetyShutdownAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/2D representations (CAD integration) for communicating system states/models to engineers/stakeholders"
    DTDFVocab:provides StateVisualizationAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Algorithmic identification of anomalies (sensor failures, performance drops) using data-model fusion for maintenance teams"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance PerformanceDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of temperature/performance metrics showing deviations from expected behavior (accuracy ±0.5°C)"
]

instance BehaviorForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted PT behavior under hypothetical scenarios (stress tests, environmental changes) with confidence metrics"
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic parameter adjustments to optimize energy efficiency/operational cost via gradient-based optimization algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActuatorCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote actuation commands to TemperatureControlActuator/AirflowActuator via Raspberry Pi interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyShutdownAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated shutdown procedures when thermal thresholds (±0.5°C) are violated"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance StateVisualizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generating interactive 3D/2D visualizations of temperature distribution and system states for engineering review"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ParameterEstimationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Updating model parameters via Python calibration algorithms using real-time sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected anomalies in system performance metrics using data-model fusion"
]

instance ModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Refinement service updating model parameters based on calibration algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelRefinementService
]

instance DataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating data from various sources for unified processing"
]

instance BehaviorSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service simulating system behavior under various conditions"
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureControlActuator, AirflowActuator,
                 PermafrostTemperatureSensor, TemperatureDataStream,
                 PhysicalThermalModel, StateEstimationModel, SimulationEngineModel,
                 TemperatureDataStore, RabbitMQCommunicationEnabler,
                 PythonCalibrationEnabler, ControlInterfaceEnabler,
                 MonitoringService, OptimizationService, PredictionService,
                 ControlService, VisualizationService, FaultDetectionService,
                 StateEstimationService, ModelRefinementService,
                 BehaviorSimulationService, DataIntegrationService
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system in the provided context emphasizes real-time synchronization between the Physical Twin (PT) and its digital counterpart through continuous data collection via sensors. The temporal aspects involve **calibration algorithms** that update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment with **best-estimate parameters** in near real-time, ensuring alignment with the PT's operational conditions. While specific synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, or granularity needs are not quantified, the DT relies on **continuous monitoring** and **adaptive algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to maintain temporal coherence. The system prioritizes **low-latency data processing** to enable timely decision-making for safety and optimization, though exact time scales (e.g., milliseconds for control loops) are not specified in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **unified architecture pattern** where the DT serves as a cohesive framework integrating **modelling, simulation, monitoring, and optimisation** technologies. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to dynamically update models based on real-time sensor data from the PT, ensuring alignment with physical behavior. Resource management is implicit through the use of **state estimators, fault detectors, and supervisory controllers**, which operate in a closed-loop manner to maintain system performance. System-wide coordination is achieved via **centralized decision-making** (e.g., human or algorithmic interventions) triggered by violations detected in monitoring processes. No explicit mention of orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, cloud-native architectures) or inter-DT coordination is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle encompasses phases where models evolve from conceptual design to operational deployment and continuous refinement. Initially, during **development**, the DT integrates **abstract representations** (e.g., CAD models for physical attributes like wheel sizes or chassis shapes) alongside **dynamic models** (e.g., differential equations for system dynamics). These models are calibrated to align with the Physical Twin (PT) via **calibration algorithms** that update parameters based on sensor data. The lifecycle transitions through stages where:
    - **Representation types** shift from static (e.g., CAD) to dynamic (e.g., simulation-based) models.
    - **Transition management** involves seamless integration of new data and models, ensuring consistency between cyber and physical domains.
    The lifecycle governance approach emphasizes **continuous integration** of models, simulations, and monitoring into the DT framework, ensuring they remain relevant post-deployment. Once deployed, DTs are not discarded but evolve through iterative updates driven by real-time data and algorithmic adjustments (e.g., fault detection, state estimation).
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
  The DT prioritizes **high-fidelity models** by integrating **calibration algorithms** that iteratively update parameters (e.g., system dynamics) based on real-time sensor data. This ensures the digital representation closely mirrors the Physical Twin’s behavior under current conditions.

  **Validation and Verification Methods:**
  Fidelity is implicitly validated through **closed-loop feedback**: sensor data feeds into models, which are recalibrated and validated against actual PT performance. The absence of explicit methods (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations, experimental validation) suggests reliance on **operational consistency**—the DT’s predictive accuracy is demonstrated by its ability to replicate PT behavior during deployment.

  **Uncertainty Quantification:**
  Uncertainty is managed via **calibration-driven refinement**, where sensor-derived data reduces parameter estimation errors. The document does not specify formal uncertainty quantification techniques (e.g., Bayesian inference), but the iterative calibration process implies a **confidence-driven approach** to model validity.

  **Error Detection and Correction:**
  Errors are detected by **monitoring algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors) that flag deviations from optimal/system-safe thresholds. Corrections are automated (via algorithms) or human-guided, ensuring the DT’s models remain aligned with the PT’s evolving state.

  **Quality Assurance Processes:**
  The DT’s quality is ensured through **continuous calibration** and **operational validation**, where model accuracy is implicitly verified by real-world deployment success. No explicit QA frameworks (e.g., ISO standards) are mentioned, but the emphasis on **real-time adaptation** underscores a proactive approach to maintaining fidelity.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin’s technical connection** is implemented via a **RabbitMQ server** hosted on the **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, acting as a centralized message broker. The network topology is **single-node** (localized to the Pi), with no explicit mention of distributed architectures or multi-node redundancy. Communication protocols are **RabbitMQ’s AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol)**, though no specific latency or bandwidth requirements are quantified. Security protocols are **not explicitly documented**; the system’s reliance on the embedded calibration loop for data integrity implies minimal external security measures (e.g., no encryption or authentication for sensor data). The infrastructure is deterministic in operation, as the Pi’s low-level drivers and calibration algorithms enforce strict timing and parameter consistency.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin’s hosting and deployment** infrastructure is deployed on a **single-node, on-premise Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, leveraging its embedded hardware capabilities for real-time processing. Computational requirements are met by the Pi’s CPU and onboard storage (no explicit specs provided), with no mention of cloud-edge hybrid or distributed deployment models. Scalability is limited to the Pi’s hardware constraints, as the system does not support multi-node expansion or dynamic workload balancing. Availability/reliability is ensured by the **calibration algorithms**, which iteratively refine model parameters to minimize discrepancies between physical and digital twins. The deployment model is **fixed-configuration**, with no mention of agile or containerized deployment mechanisms.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework integrates **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment using real-time sensor data to ensure parameter accuracy. Safety is enforced through **monitoring systems** that continuously validate the system's operational compliance with predefined safety constraints. Violations trigger automated or human-driven interventions to maintain safe and optimal performance. While no explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication, or threat mitigation) is provided, the framework implicitly relies on **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., state estimators and supervisory controllers) to prevent unsafe states by enforcing constraints on the Physical Twin’s behavior. The core emphasis is on **real-time safety verification** via model-based monitoring rather than detailed security protocols."
]
